---
title: plot.visualization
---



`plot.visualization`



## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [plot_design_points](#spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_design_points) | Plot design points projected onto two selected dimensions. |
| [plot_important_hyperparameter_contour](#spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_important_hyperparameter_contour) | Plot surrogate contours for all combinations of the top max_imp important parameters. |
| [plot_progress](#spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_progress) | Plot optimization progress showing all evaluations and best-so-far curve. |
| [plot_surrogate](#spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_surrogate) | Plot the surrogate model for two dimensions. |

### plot_design_points { #spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_design_points }

```python
plot.visualization.plot_design_points(
    X,
    i=0,
    j=1,
    agg='mean',
    show=True,
    **kwargs,
)
```

Plot design points projected onto two selected dimensions.

Displays a scatter plot of design points using dimensions ``i`` and ``j``
as the axes. When ``X`` has more than two dimensions, all remaining
dimensions (≠ ``i`` and ≠ ``j``) are aggregated per point using the
``agg`` function, and the result is used to colour the markers.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                     | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Default    |
|----------|------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X        | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | Design matrix of shape ``(n_samples, n_dim)``. Each row is one design point.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | _required_ |
| i        | [int](`int`)                             | Index of the dimension to plot on the x-axis. Defaults to ``0``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | `0`        |
| j        | [int](`int`)                             | Index of the dimension to plot on the y-axis. Defaults to ``1``. Must differ from ``i`` when ``n_dim > 1``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | `1`        |
| agg      | [str](`str`) or [callable](`callable`)   | Aggregation applied to the dimensions that are *not* plotted (i.e. every column except ``i`` and ``j``). Supported strings: ``"mean"``, ``"median"``, ``"min"``, ``"max"``. A callable ``f(arr, axis)`` is also accepted (e.g. ``np.std``). The aggregated value is used as the colour of each marker so that spread across the hidden dimensions is visible at a glance. When ``n_dim == 2`` this parameter is ignored (no hidden dims). Defaults to ``"mean"``. | `'mean'`   |
| show     | [bool](`bool`)                           | If ``True``, call :func:`matplotlib.pyplot.show` before returning. Set to ``False`` when running inside tests or scripts that manage display manually. Defaults to ``True``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `True`     |
| **kwargs | [object](`object`)                       | Additional keyword arguments forwarded to :func:`matplotlib.pyplot.subplots` (e.g. ``figsize``) and to :func:`matplotlib.axes.Axes.scatter` (e.g. ``s``, ``cmap``, ``alpha``).  ``figsize`` is intercepted and passed to ``subplots``; all other keys go to ``scatter``.                                                                                                                                                                                          | `{}`       |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type               | Description                                                      |
|--------|--------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [object](`object`) | matplotlib.figure.Figure: The created figure object. Call        |
|        | [object](`object`) | ``plt.show()`` or ``fig.savefig(...)`` on the returned object as |
|        | [object](`object`) | needed.                                                          |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                         | Description                                                                                             |
|--------|------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ImportError](`ImportError`) | If *matplotlib* is not installed.                                                                       |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)   | If ``X`` is not a 2-D array, has fewer than two columns, or if ``i`` / ``j`` are out of range or equal. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#e8dc2ff3 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.function import sphere
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_design_points

opt = SpotOptim(
    fun=sphere,
    bounds=[(-5, 5), (-5, 5)],
    n_initial=10,
    seed=42,
)
# Generate a Latin-hypercube initial design (in original scale)
X0 = opt.get_initial_design()

# 2-D design – simple scatter on both dimensions
fig = plot_design_points(X0, i=0, j=1, figsize=(5, 4))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=469 height=373}
:::
:::


::: {#6f7ba478 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.function import sphere
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_design_points

opt = SpotOptim(
    fun=sphere,
    bounds=[(-5, 5)] * 4,
    n_initial=20,
    seed=42,
)
X0 = opt.get_initial_design()

# 4-D design – colour encodes the mean of the two hidden dimensions
fig = plot_design_points(X0, i=0, j=1, agg="mean", figsize=(6, 5), s=60)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization_files/figure-html/cell-3-output-1.png){width=557 height=469}
:::
:::


### plot_important_hyperparameter_contour { #spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_important_hyperparameter_contour }

```python
plot.visualization.plot_important_hyperparameter_contour(
    optimizer,
    max_imp=3,
    show=True,
    alpha=0.8,
    cmap='jet',
    num=100,
    add_points=True,
    grid_visible=True,
    contour_levels=30,
    figsize=(12, 10),
)
```

Plot surrogate contours for all combinations of the top max_imp important parameters.

This method identifies the most important parameters using importance scores,
then generates surrogate contour plots for all pairwise combinations of these
parameters. Factor (categorical) variables are handled by creating discrete grids
and displaying factor level names on the axes.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                                                             | Description                                                                                                           | Default    |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| optimizer      | [SpotOptimProtocol](`spotoptim.core.protocol.SpotOptimProtocol`) | SpotOptim instance containing optimization data.                                                                      | _required_ |
| max_imp        | [int](`int`)                                                     | Number of most important parameters to visualize. Defaults to 3. For max_imp=3, creates 3 plots: (0,1), (0,2), (1,2). | `3`        |
| show           | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, displays plots immediately. Defaults to True.                                                                | `True`     |
| alpha          | [float](`float`)                                                 | Transparency of 3D surface plots (0=transparent, 1=opaque). Defaults to 0.8.                                          | `0.8`      |
| cmap           | [str](`str`)                                                     | Matplotlib colormap name. Defaults to 'jet'.                                                                          | `'jet'`    |
| num            | [int](`int`)                                                     | Number of grid points per dimension. Defaults to 100. For factor variables, uses the number of unique levels instead. | `100`      |
| add_points     | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, overlay evaluated points on contour plots. Defaults to True.                                                 | `True`     |
| grid_visible   | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, show grid lines. Defaults to True.                                                                           | `True`     |
| contour_levels | [int](`int`)                                                     | Number of contour levels. Defaults to 30.                                                                             | `30`       |
| figsize        | tuple of int                                                     | Figure size in inches (width, height). Defaults to (12, 10).                                                          | `(12, 10)` |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                |
|--------|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If optimization hasn't been run yet or max_imp is invalid. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#1bbb0e32 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_important_hyperparameter_contour
from spotoptim.function import sphere
# Initialize and run optimizer with enough dimensions (here 4)
opt = SpotOptim(fun=sphere, bounds=[(-5, 5)]*4,
                 max_iter=10, n_initial=5,
                 var_name=['temp', 'pressure', 'velocity', 'acceleration'])
result = opt.optimize()
# Plot contours for top 3 important hyperparameters
plot_important_hyperparameter_contour(opt, max_imp=3, show=False)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Plotting surrogate contours for top 3 most important parameters:
  velocity: importance = 54.93% (type: float)
  acceleration: importance = 30.58% (type: float)
  temp: importance = 8.72% (type: float)

Generating 3 surrogate plots...
  Plotting velocity vs acceleration
  Plotting velocity vs temp
  Plotting acceleration vs temp
```

### plot_progress { #spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_progress }

```python
plot.visualization.plot_progress(
    optimizer,
    show=True,
    log_y=False,
    figsize=(10, 6),
    ylabel='Objective Value',
    mo=False,
)
```

Plot optimization progress showing all evaluations and best-so-far curve.

This method visualizes the optimization history, displaying both individual
function evaluations and the cumulative best value found. Initial design points
are shown as individual scatter points with a light grey background region,
while sequential optimization iterations are connected with lines.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                                                             | Description                                                            | Default             |
|-----------|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
| optimizer | [SpotOptimProtocol](`spotoptim.core.protocol.SpotOptimProtocol`) | SpotOptim instance containing optimization data.                       | _required_          |
| show      | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | Whether to display the plot. Defaults to True.                         | `True`              |
| log_y     | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | Whether to use log scale for y-axis. Defaults to False.                | `False`             |
| figsize   | [tuple](`tuple`)                                                 | Figure size as (width, height). Defaults to (10, 6).                   | `(10, 6)`           |
| ylabel    | [str](`str`)                                                     | Label for y-axis. Defaults to "Objective Value".                       | `'Objective Value'` |
| mo        | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | Whether to plot individual objectives if available. Defaults to False. | `False`             |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                         | Description                          |
|--------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)   | If optimization hasn't been run yet. |
|        | [ImportError](`ImportError`) | If matplotlib is not installed.      |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#4f26e763 .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_progress
from spotoptim.function import sphere
opt = SpotOptim(fun=sphere, bounds=[(-5, 5)]*2,
                max_iter=10, n_initial=5, seed=42)
result = opt.optimize()
# Plot optimization progress (linear scale)
plot_progress(opt, log_y=False, show=False)
# Plot with log scale
plot_progress(opt, log_y=True, show=False)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization_files/figure-html/cell-5-output-1.png){width=949 height=565}
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization_files/figure-html/cell-5-output-2.png){width=948 height=565}
:::
:::


### plot_surrogate { #spotoptim.plot.visualization.plot_surrogate }

```python
plot.visualization.plot_surrogate(
    optimizer,
    i=0,
    j=1,
    show=True,
    alpha=0.8,
    var_name=None,
    cmap='jet',
    num=100,
    vmin=None,
    vmax=None,
    add_points=True,
    grid_visible=True,
    contour_levels=30,
    figsize=(12, 10),
)
```

Plot the surrogate model for two dimensions.

Creates a 2x2 plot showing:
- Top left: 3D surface of predictions
- Top right: 3D surface of prediction uncertainty
- Bottom left: Contour plot of predictions with evaluated points
- Bottom right: Contour plot of prediction uncertainty

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                                                             | Description                                                                      | Default    |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| optimizer      | [SpotOptimProtocol](`spotoptim.core.protocol.SpotOptimProtocol`) | SpotOptim instance containing optimization data and surrogate model.             | _required_ |
| i              | [int](`int`)                                                     | Index of the first dimension to plot. Defaults to 0.                             | `0`        |
| j              | [int](`int`)                                                     | Index of the second dimension to plot. Defaults to 1.                            | `1`        |
| show           | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, displays the plot immediately. Defaults to True.                        | `True`     |
| alpha          | [float](`float`)                                                 | Transparency of the 3D surface plots (0=transparent, 1=opaque). Defaults to 0.8. | `0.8`      |
| var_name       | list of str                                                      | Names for each dimension. If None, uses instance var_name. Defaults to None.     | `None`     |
| cmap           | [str](`str`)                                                     | Matplotlib colormap name. Defaults to 'jet'.                                     | `'jet'`    |
| num            | [int](`int`)                                                     | Number of grid points per dimension for mesh grid. Defaults to 100.              | `100`      |
| vmin           | [float](`float`)                                                 | Minimum value for color scale. If None, determined from data. Defaults to None.  | `None`     |
| vmax           | [float](`float`)                                                 | Maximum value for color scale. If None, determined from data. Defaults to None.  | `None`     |
| add_points     | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, overlay evaluated points on contour plots. Defaults to True.            | `True`     |
| grid_visible   | [bool](`bool`)                                                   | If True, show grid lines on contour plots. Defaults to True.                     | `True`     |
| contour_levels | [int](`int`)                                                     | Number of contour levels. Defaults to 30.                                        | `30`       |
| figsize        | tuple of int                                                     | Figure size in inches (width, height). Defaults to (12, 10).                     | `(12, 10)` |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                         | Description                                                  |
|--------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)   | If optimization hasn't been run yet, or if i, j are invalid. |
|        | [ImportError](`ImportError`) | If matplotlib is not installed.                              |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#4174a559 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_surrogate
from spotoptim.function import sphere
opt = SpotOptim(fun=sphere,
                bounds=[(-5, 5), (-5, 5)],
                max_iter=10,
                n_initial=5,
                var_name=['x1', 'x2'])
result = opt.optimize()
# Plot surrogate model for dimensions 0 and 1
plot_surrogate(opt, i=0, j=1, show=False)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization_files/figure-html/cell-6-output-1.png){width=1126 height=950}
:::
:::
